response:
Summary — what azr-compliance-v3 does for Event Grid remediation

High level
- azr-compliance-v3 can ensure (create/configure) Event Grid subscriptions for customer subscriptions that are in scope for Rackspace-managed monitoring. Remediation is exposed as both per-subscription “Ensure Event Grid” actions and a bulk “Ensure All Deployable” flow, with UI status badges and a “Latest Bulk Remediation” panel.

Inventory & scope
- The system distinguishes “deployable” subscriptions (monitoring-enabled and Rackspace-managed) from out-of-scope subscriptions. GetEventGridDeployments returns metadata.coverage (deployable_subscriptions, out_of_scope_subscriptions, deployable_missing_event_grid_count, etc.).
- SyncEventGrid scopes inventory to Rackspace-managed monitoring subscriptions only (joins customer_subscriptions -> lighthouse_delegations and requires managed_by_tenant_id = Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state ∈ {active,succeeded}). It filters Event Grid rows to Rackspace naming/destination patterns so only Rackspace-owned deployments are remediable.

Single-subscription remediation flow
- The UI initiates an ensure-call for a subscription. The frontend calls a remediation endpoint rather than directly mutating Azure resources:
  - Current deployed flows use a backend endpoint POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure (the frontend no longer calls Fleet directly in some deployments because Fleet requires application permission).
- The backend calls the Fleet/APIM Event Grid ensure API:
  - APIM path used: https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription
  - The backend obtains an authorization token and sends Authorization: Bearer to APIM. (Auth implementation changed over time: earlier browser-side MSAL call, then Entra client credentials, and later the backend reuses the Rackspace Identity flow via shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') using RACKSPACE_USERNAME/RACKSPACE_PASSWORD.)
- The backend polls the returned statusUrl until the runtimeStatus is terminal.
- Success and error mappings used by the UI/backend: succeeded and already_exists are treated as success; 404 -> mapped to not_found; 409 -> not_eligible.

Bulk remediation flow
- There is a dedicated bulk remediation implementation (design choice: do not reuse sync_queue):
  - Two Postgres tables: event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items to persist runs and per-item statuses.
  - API endpoints: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk to start a run and GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest to poll the latest run state.
  - Starting a bulk run snapshots the full set of deployable subscriptions from the shared Event Grid coverage SQL and will reuse an active queued/running run if present.
  - A dedicated timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation runs every ~15 seconds, guarded by its own advisory lock, and drives the work. The latest-run item statuses are persisted so the UI can refresh and show steady state.
  - The EventGridDetails client POSTs once to start bulk remediation and then polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges item statuses into deployable cards and shows the Latest Bulk Remediation panel.

UI behavior
- EventGridDetails shows explicit remediation buckets (“Ready For Event Grid Deployment” and “Monitoring Required First”) based on metadata.coverage.
- UI actions: per-subscription Ensure Event Grid, Ensure All Deployable (bulk), and local remediation state badges. The client polls statusUrl / bulk latest endpoints and maps terminal states and error conditions as above.

Operational notes
- Sync and remediation intentionally use a single Rackspace Lighthouse credential for inventory and do not acquire per-customer tenant tokens.
- SyncEventGrid filters out unmanaged subscriptions and records stats for skipped/unmanaged/stale items so remediation only targets Rackspace-managed Event Grid subscriptions.

If you want, I can sketch the sequence diagram for single vs bulk remediation or list exact DB schema fields and worker lock behavior.

Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest-run item statuses for page refresh continuity. EventGridDetails now calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk once, polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges latest item statuses into visible deployable cards, and shows a Latest Bulk Remediation panel.
[specifics: application: azr-compliance-v3; postgres_tables: event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs, event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items; worker: ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation (15s timer, uses its own advisory lock); endpoints: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk, GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest; sync_queue_reuse: design choice = do not reuse sync_queue; bulk_start_behavior: reuses an active queued/running run and snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL; status_persistence: latest-run item statuses persisted for page-refresh continuity; EventGridDetails_client: issues POST once then polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges item statuses into deployable cards and shows Latest Bulk Remediation panel]", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL, polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus, treats succeeded/already_exists as success, maps 404 to not_found, and maps 409 to not_eligible. UI adds per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges.
[specifics: app: azr-compliance-v3; remediation_call: browser-side Fleet Platform call (instead of backend proxy); auth_reason: browser has operator MSAL Bearer token; backend behind APIM may not retain raw Authorization credential; metadata_field: metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi); env_var_scope: VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE (optional Fleet-specific scope requested); api_call: POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL; polling_mechanism: polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus; success_conditions: treats succeeded and already_exists as success; error_mappings: 404 -> not_found; 409 -> not_eligible; ui_changes: per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges]", "2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.
[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation; credential_type: ClientSecretCredential; token_scope: api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default; apim_subscription_endpoint: https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription; frontend_request: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure; reason_for_backend_flow: Fleet requires application permission; backend_behavior: polls returned statusUrl until completion; previous_flow: browser-side Fleet API call]", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, redesigned Event Grid Details to distinguish deployment inventory from remediation coverage. GetEventGridDeployments now returns metadata.coverage with deployable_missing_event_grid_count, missing_monitoring_count, and arrays for deployable_subscriptions vs out_of_scope_subscriptions, derived from active customer subscriptions, monitoring entitlement, and Rackspace-managed Event Grid inventory. The frontend EventGridDetails page now shows explicit remediation buckets: 'Ready For Event Grid Deployment' for monitoring-enabled subscriptions missing Event Grid, and 'Monitoring Required First' for subscriptions outside monitoring scope. This resolves ambiguity in the prior 'Subscriptions Without Event Grid' card, which only reflected monitoring-entitled scope.
[specifics: resource: azr-compliance-v3; GetEventGridDeployments: returns metadata.coverage; metadata.coverage.field: deployable_missing_event_grid_count; metadata.coverage.field: missing_monitoring_count; metadata.coverage.array: deployable_subscriptions; metadata.coverage.array: out_of_scope_subscriptions; frontend_page: EventGridDetails; remediation_bucket: Ready For Event Grid Deployment; remediation_bucket: Monitoring Required First; prior_card_behavior: 'Subscriptions Without Event Grid' only reflected monitoring-entitled scope]", "2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Fleet Event Grid remediation token acquisition was revised again on 2026-04-09: instead of Entra client credentials, backend-py now reuses the existing Rackspace Identity flow backed by RACKSPACE_USERNAME and RACKSPACE_PASSWORD. shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py calls shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') and sends that token as Authorization: Bearer to APIM for the targeted Event Grid ensure endpoint.
[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3; component: backend-py switched auth from Entra client credentials to Rackspace Identity flow; env_vars: RACKSPACE_USERNAME, RACKSPACE_PASSWORD; caller_file: shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py; called_function: shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity'); auth_mode: identity; auth_header: Authorization: Bearer; target_endpoint: APIM Event Grid ensure endpoint]", "2026-04-09: For azr-compliance-v3 PR #72 on 2026-04-09, two live GitHub review threads on GetEventGridDeployments were valid and fixed in commit 633573dc on develop. Fixes: (1) summary query no longer prepends an extra WITH before EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE, preventing invalid SQL 'WITH WITH ...'; (2) selected tenant_id now uses COALESCE(eegd.tenant_id, mms.tenant_id, cs.tenant_id) to match join/filter fallback behavior. Added unit assertions to catch both regressions, reran ruff and focused pytest, replied to both threads, and resolved them.
[specifics: repository: azr-compliance-v3; pr_number: 72; commit: 633573dc; branch: develop; function: GetEventGridDeployments; sql_cte: EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE; sql_error: 'WITH WITH' caused by extra WITH prepended before EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE; tenant_id_coalesce: COALESCE(eegd.tenant_id, mms.tenant_id, cs.tenant_id); tests: added unit assertions; reran ruff and focused pytest]", "2026-04-08: On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upsert by a shared ownership classifier: event subscription name must match Rackspace naming conventions (rax-/rackspace-) and destination must match Rackspace-managed webhook/resource patterns (rackspace.com, compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts, or known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs). Sync stats now distinguish skipped unmanaged subscriptions, stale/missing subscriptions from SubscriptionNotFound warnings, and filtered unmanaged deployments. GetEventGridDeployments summary and list queries now use the same managed monitoring scope so page KPIs reflect only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscript
[specifics: environment: azr-compliance-v3; sync_component: SyncEventGrid; scoping_rule: join customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations requiring managed_by_tenant_id = Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in [active,succeeded]; credential_usage: single Rackspace Lighthouse credential (no per-customer tenant tokens); event_subscription_name_pattern: rax-/rackspace-; destination_patterns: rackspace.com; compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts; known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs; GetEventGridDeployments_scope: summary and list use same managed monitoring scope (only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscriptions); sync_stats_categories: skipped_unmanaged_subscriptions; stale_or_missing (SubscriptionNotFound warnings); filtered_unmanaged_deployments]"]}
